actionable_feedback: Normalize CSV header names to the canonical field names used by verifiers (suggested mappings: data -> data_classification, remediation_order -> recommended_remediation_order, row_value_citations -> evidence_reference, review_status -> notes).

Embed or attach primary evidence items referenced in evidence_needed (e.g., scan outputs, admin assignment record, data classification proof) or provide stable links to them; then update validation_checks entries from NEEDS_REVIEW to PASS where evidence is present.

Add a short README (or metadata row) documenting the scoring formula (risk_score = 20 + 80*critical_rule_match + 30*findings_over_5), column definitions, and assumptions so reviewers and automated tools can unambiguously interpret the CSV.

Include a simple verification script (python or shell) that recomputes risk scores from the CSV inputs and fails if any computed_risk_score cells differ; include running this script in the trace/step logs so verifiers can reproduce recomputation automatically.

Update grader/verifier messaging to explicitly state the scope of deterministic precheck (artifact presence/contract) versus model-based arithmetic/logic checks so that downstream reviewers understand what was and was not SME-reviewed.
artifact_specific_comments: CSV includes a clear score_formula and per-row intermediate_calculations; computed_risk_score values are consistent with the formula.
Validation_checks column appropriately flags NEEDS_REVIEW where original evidence is not embedded; turning these into PASS by attaching evidence will strengthen semantic grounding.
feedback_summary: Deliverable artifact access_policy_simulation.csv meets the artifact-contract and arithmetic/logic checks: scoring formula and intermediate calculations are present and recompute to the computed_risk_score values (A=100, B=50, C=20). Deterministic artifact precheck passed and model-based grader verified consistency. Main improvement opportunities: (1) normalize CSV header names to the expected canonical fields for automated verifiers, and (2) embed or attach original evidence documents (scan outputs, admin assignment records, classification proofs) instead of only marking needs_review, to improve semantic validation and reviewer efficiency. Also clarify grader messaging to separate deterministic precheck scope from model-based arithmetic verification.
